docs.title: AedtToolbox 帮助文档
docs.text: 欢迎使用 AedtToolbox — 一个为 Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT) 设计的悬浮工具栏，提供快速脚本执行和工具集成能力。

docs.title: 快速导航
docs.text:
• AedtToolbox 工具说明 工具启动、菜单操作、Python环境配置 查看文档

  • 用户自定义菜单 添加自定义菜单项、脚本和外部程序 查看文档

  • HFSS 3D Layout 工具集 叠层导入、背钻、端口排序等自动化工具 查看文档

  • Toolkits Q2D参数化传输线生成、PyAEDT面板安装等高级工具 查看文档

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Via->AutoBackdrill

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 自动设定过孔的背钻，支持指定网络名称和Stub长度。仅支持2023R1及之后的版本。 自动判断背钻的方向。

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text:
确保3D Layout工程处于选中状态，然后在Toolbox上右键，选择 Via->AutoBackdrill. 会弹出AutoBackdrill设定对话框

  • Net Match(Regrex)：使用正则表达式匹配需要背钻的网络，默认.*匹配所有网络。
  • Backdrill Stub: 背钻精度，默认8mil

docs.title: Net Match(Regrex)
docs.text:
• 正则表达式使用.匹配任意多字符，比如使用DQ.匹配以DQ开头的Nets.
  • 正则表达式的写法请参考 https://www.runoob.com/regexp/regexp-syntax.html

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text: • 支持总线的书写方法，比如使用DQ[15:0]匹配 DQ0-DQ15的网络。（注：这种写法并不是Regrex的一部分）

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Net->AutoXNet

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 当信号线经过电容并且一侧的网络没有使用命名（网络为数字或者随机名称），本脚本可以根据一侧已经命名的网络，对另外一侧进行命名，新命名的网络会根据RLC的不同添加_C,_R,_L的标志。（当前默认只对Cap）

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 确保3D Layout工程处于选中状态，然后在Toolbox上右键，选择 Net->AutoXNet. 脚本会自动在当前激活的AEDT里面执行，检测电容两侧的网络，对于未命名的网络进行命名。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
• 脚本会直接跳过3D Layout里面定义的Power/GND网络。
  • Cap需要分配到Component的Capacitors里面

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Export->ExportToHfssWithNets

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 将3D Layout中的3D模型导出到HFSS后，可以按照Net对物体进行分组，这样更便于查看和设定物体。

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 确保3D Layout工程处于选中状态，并且已经设定完成（叠层，端口，Cutout等），确保设置了HFSS Setup(建议最好只有1个setup)。然后在Toolbox上右键，选择Export->ExportToHfssWithNets

docs.title: 完成效果
docs.text: 等待脚本执行完成，最终效果如下：

docs.title: Overlapping Stackup的支持
docs.text: 2.5D/3DIC Package在3D Layout中使用了Overlapping叠层，ExportToHfssWithNets命令可以支持Overlapping的导出。但是如果有2个及以上的金属层位于同一高度时，对Nets的识别会存在一些问题。

docs.title: Interposer 导出案例：
docs.text:
1. Overlapping Stackup 且所有金属层处于不同高度
  2. 导出之前Design Mode需要使用General Mode，不然脚本执行会出错。
  3. 导出效果

docs.title: 特别说明
docs.text: 3D Layout使用HFSS求解器，可以和HFSS精度保持一致，设置和操作上要比HFSS 3D方便和快速。大部分场景下并不建议把3D Layout导出HFSS 3D进行求解。

docs.title: 功能概述
docs.text:
HfssAutoSetupGUI 是一个面向 AEDT 3D Layout / HFSS Layout 工作流的图形化配置选择工具。它通过读取脚本目录下 config 文件夹中的 CSV 模板，让用户先选择业务类型，再选择具体配置档，最后自动把对应的 HFSS 求解设置和 Airbox 参数应用到当前设计。

这个工具的目标不是手工逐项填写 Setup，而是把常用仿真配置模板化，减少重复配置时间，并保证团队内部的设置口径一致。

docs.title: 适用场景
docs.text:
• 为 SerDes、DDRx 等高速通道快速套用标准 HFSS Setup
  • 统一 sweep 范围、收敛策略、Airbox 尺寸等常见参数
  • 降低手动建 Setup 时的遗漏和配置差异

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text:
3DLayout -> Setup -> HfssAutoSetupGUI

对应菜单配置如下：

<SubMenu Type="MenuItem"
         Name="HfssAutoSetupGUI"
         ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib/3DLayout/Setup/HfssAutoSetupGUI.py"
         Arguments=""
         PythonPath=""></SubMenu>

docs.title: 工具位置
docs.text:
工具位于 Toolbox 安装目录下：

Toolbox安装目录/
├── UserLib/
│   └── 3DLayout/
│       └── Setup/
│           ├── HfssAutoSetupGUI.py   （GUI 主程序）
│           ├── autoSetup.py          （自动设置执行逻辑）
│           └── config/
│               ├── DDRx.csv
│               └── Serdes.csv
└── ...

docs.title: 界面说明
docs.text:
启动工具后会弹出一个 Windows Forms 窗口，主要由以下区域组成：

  • Select Type: 选择配置类别，对应 config 目录下的 CSV 文件名
  • Select Config: 选择该类别下的具体配置名，对应 CSV 第一行中的配置列
  • Config Preview: 预览当前配置的键值内容
  • OK: 将当前配置应用到设计
  • Cancel: 关闭窗口，不执行任何操作

docs.title: 当前已识别的配置类型
docs.text:
根据现有脚本目录，工具默认可加载以下类型：

  • Serdes
  • DDRx

如果后续在 config 目录增加新的 CSV 文件，工具会自动把文件名加入 Select Type 下拉框，无需额外修改 GUI 代码。

docs.title: 输入来源
docs.text:
工具不要求用户手动输入仿真参数，所有参数都来自 CSV 配置模板。

CSV 的组织方式如下：

  • 第一行定义配置名称列表
  • 第一列定义参数键名
  • 其余单元格定义每个配置对应的参数值

例如：

  • Setup/Name
  • SolutionType
  • AdaptiveFrequency
  • Order
  • DeltaS
  • Setup/Sweep/Name
  • SweepType
  • InterpolatingTolerance
  • SweepData
  • UseQ3DForDC
  • Airbox/Options/AirHorExt/Ext

docs.title: 输出结果
docs.text:
点击 OK 后，工具会把当前选中的配置转换成参数字典，并传给 HfssAutoSetup(data).run() 执行。脚本的实际输出包括：

  • 初始化当前 AEDT 布局设计上下文
  • 应用 HFSS 求解设置
  • 应用频率扫描设置
  • 应用 Airbox 设置
  • 关闭窗口并提示执行完成

docs.title: 用户操作步骤
docs.text:
1. 确保当前打开的是可用于 HFSS 分析的 3D Layout 设计
  2. 在 Toolbox 菜单中启动 HfssAutoSetupGUI
  3. 在 Select Type 中选择业务类型，例如 Serdes 或 DDRx
  4. 在 Select Config 中选择目标配置，例如某个速率档位
  5. 在 Config Preview 中确认将要写入的参数
  6. 点击 OK 应用配置

docs.title: 执行流程
docs.text:
启动 HfssAutoSetupGUI
    ↓
扫描 config 目录中的 CSV 文件
    ↓
填充 Select Type 下拉框
    ↓
根据所选类型读取 CSV 表头
    ↓
填充 Select Config 下拉框
    ↓
解析所选配置列为参数字典
    ↓
在预览区显示配置内容
    ↓
点击 OK
    ↓
调用 HfssAutoSetup(data)
    ↓
初始化当前 Layout 设计
    ↓
执行 solveSetup()
    ↓
执行 setAirbox()
    ↓
完成自动设置

docs.title: Serdes.csv
docs.text:
Serdes.csv 中提供了一组按速率区分的 SerDes 仿真模板，例如：

  • SERDES_8G
  • SERDES_16G
  • SERDES_25G
  • SERDES_32G
  • SERDES_56G
  • SERDES_112G
  • SERDES_224G
  • SERDES_448G

这些模板通常会预设：

  • 多个自适应频点
  • 插值型 Sweep
  • 高频范围下的线性采样步长
  • 固定的 Airbox 水平和上下延展量

docs.title: DDRx.csv
docs.text:
DDRx.csv 中提供了一组 DDR 相关模板，例如：

  • DDRx_3200
  • DDRx_6400
  • DDRx_10700
  • DDRx_17600

这类模板通常会根据速率档位调整：

  • 自适应频率
  • Sweep 终止频率
  • Basis Order
  • 是否启用 UseQ3DForDC

docs.title: 预览区说明
docs.text:
预览区会把当前配置按 键 : 值 的形式显示出来，便于在执行前确认。

显示规则如下：

  • 顶部显示当前 Type 和 Config
  • 后续按键名排序显示所有可用参数
  • 仅展示当前配置列中有值的项目

这意味着如果某个参数在该配置列为空，它不会被写入当前配置字典，也不会显示在预览区。

docs.title: 实际计算流程
docs.text:
HfssAutoSetupGUI.py 本身主要负责界面和 CSV 解析，真正执行设置的是 autoSetup.py 中的 HfssAutoSetup 类。

根据当前脚本实现，run() 的有效执行步骤主要包括：

  1. 初始化 Layout() 并绑定当前设计
  2. 调用 solveSetup() 写入求解设置与扫频设置
  3. 调用 setAirbox() 写入 Airbox 参数

脚本中还保留了若干被注释掉的扩展流程，例如：

  • Cutout
  • Nets 配置
  • 器件模型配置
  • Backdrill
  • Pin Group
  • Solder Ball
  • Ports
  • Source
  • Geometry Check
  • Analyze

当前 GUI 版本默认不会自动执行这些步骤，也不会自动启动求解。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
1. 该工具依赖 config 目录下的 CSV 文件，若目录不存在或为空，界面将无法加载配置项
  2. 工具应用的是模板参数，不会自动检查这些参数是否完全适配当前板级结构
  3. 当前实现主要自动处理 Setup 和 Airbox，不等同于完整的一键建模与仿真流程
  4. 若 CSV 中使用了脚本未识别的键名，后台会记录 key not found 警告
  5. 在执行前应确认当前活动设计已经满足 HFSS 3D Layout 分析前提条件

docs.title: 故障排查
docs.text: 问题 可能原因 处理建议 打开窗口后类型列表为空 config 目录不存在或没有 CSV 文件 检查 UserLib/3DLayout/Setup/config/ 目录 选择类型后没有配置名 CSV 第一行未正确填写配置名称 检查表头格式，确认第一行第二列开始为配置名 预览区为空 当前配置列没有有效键值对 检查 CSV 对应列是否为空 点击 OK 后报错 当前设计上下文不正确，或配置键不兼容 确认在正确的 AEDT 设计中执行，并检查 CSV 键名 设置结果与预期不一致 模板参数不匹配当前项目 在预览区确认参数后，再针对项目调整 CSV 模板

docs.title: 相关资源
docs.text:
• HFSS 3D Layout 相关帮助
  • Toolbox 菜单配置说明: ToolBox/AddItem.md
  • 相邻工具参考: HFSS3DLayout/AutoBackdrill.md

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Stackup->ImportCSVStackup

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 通过CSV文件快速导入加工厂给定的叠层。可以把工厂提供的xlsx文件按照提供的CSV模板格式输出成文件做输入即可。列的顺序不限，不在定义内的列会被忽略。

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 需要按照提供的CSV模板填写叠层信息。然后在Toolbox上右键，选择Stackup->ImportCSVStackup，选择已经填写好的CSV叠层模板，进行导入。 在ImportCSVStackup.py目录下提供了"CsvStackupTemp_full.csv" 和 "CsvStackupTemp_simple.csv"两个模板可供使用。

docs.title: 模板格式
docs.text:
Layer Type Thickness(mil) Cond DK DF Roughness UNNAMED_000 dielectric 0 3.7 0.15 0.5um TOP signal 1.9 3.7 0.15 0.5um UNNAMED_002 dielectric 2.65 3.5 0.004 0.5um PWR signal 1.3 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_004 dielectric 50 3.5 0.004 0.5um LYR_1 signal 1.2 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_006 dielectric 8 3.5 0.004 0.5um LYR_2 signal 1.2 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_008 dielectric 8 3.5 0.004 0.5um GND signal 1.3 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_010 dielectric 2.65 3.5 0.004 0.5um BOTTOM signal 1.9 3.7 0.15 0.5um UNNAMED_012 dielectric 0 3.7 0.15 0.5um

说明： 1. Layer, Type,Thickness必须指定, 未指定的行将被忽略。 2. Cond, DK, DF如果缺失或者为给定数值，默认会使用Copper和FR4_epoxy替代 3. Thickness可以使用()指定单位，或者在数值中直接使用单位。 4. Roughness为可选属性，Groiss格式: 0.5um, huray格式: 0.5um;2.9

docs.title: 可选属性
docs.text:
• Thickness：厚度，需要带单位。或者在标题上使用全局单位，比如Thickness(mil) or Thickness(mm)
  • Material：指定系统库里面的材料名称，比如Copper，此时Cond/DK/DF被忽略
  • FillMaterial:指定系统库里面的材料名称，比如FR-4，此时Cond/DK/DF被忽略
  • Cond/DK/DF 如果指定，且未指定Material或FillMaterial，自动根据参数生成新材料
  • Roughness：指定粗糙度，Groiss格式0.5um, huray格式：0.5um;2.9 or 0.5um,2.9
  • EtchFactor: 蚀刻系数，正值为Top蚀刻，负值为Bottom蚀刻

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
• CSV中的金属层数量必须和PCB里面的相同，否则无法更新。
  • CSV的介质层数量不要求和PCB里面的相同，如果介质层数不同，CSV的介质层强制覆盖PCB中的介质层。

docs.title: 附加说明
docs.text: 此选项可以配合ExportCSVStackup使用，先快速导出CSV文件模板，然后按照现有的叠层文件信息进行修改，再使用ImportCSVStackup导入。

docs.title: 案例展示
docs.text:
1. 从加工厂获取的Excel叠层文件
  2. 使用ExportCSVStackup导出现有叠层模板
  3. 使用ImportCSVStackup导入修改后的CSV文件
  4. 日志信息

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Via->KeepHDIViaPad

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 3D Layout默认会省略HDI叠孔的中间焊盘，而实际上这些焊盘确实存在。KeepHDIViaPad脚本可以检测HDI通孔，并在每一层上添加大小相等的圆形形状，以模拟这些实际存在的焊盘。

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Port->RenameEdgePort

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 对3D Layout里面手动添加的Port进行重命名，方便识别。

docs.title: 命名规则：
docs.text:
• 如果Port位置Pin上，则以 refdes.pin.Net方式命名，比如 U1.A2.DQ0
  • 如果Port位置不在Pin上，则以网络名称进行命名

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 手动添加Port后，保持3D Layout窗口在最前面，运行Port->RenameEdgePort命令，Port会自动更新。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text: 暂时不支持对耦合的waveport进行命名，比如1个waveport包含2个及以上的terminal的情况。

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Component->DissloveInvalidRLC(<2pins) Component->deleteInvalidComponents(<2pins)

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text:
删除只存在1个有效网络的RLC器件。对Layout进行Cutout和网络删除后，部分RLC的网络被删除，处于无效状态，此命令可以清除无效的RLC器件。

  • DissloveInvalidRLC(<2pins) 只Disslove RLC器件，不删除pad
  • deleteInvalidComponents(<2pins) 删除所有小于2pins的器件(RLC和其它器件)，并删除

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 确保3D Layout工程处于选中状态，然后在Toolbox上右键，选择 Component->deleteInvalidRLC. 脚本会自动在当前激活的AEDT里面执行，检测RLC的Net状态，并删除无效的网络。

docs.title: 菜单位置
docs.text: Port->orderPorts

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text: 对3D Layout的Port按照Net进行排序，排序规则为： 1. 器件 2. 总线(Net上的数字) 3. 差分线P/N, +/-, P/M

docs.title: 操作说明
docs.text: 保持3D Layout窗口在最前面，运行Port->orderPorts命令。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text: 暂时不支持对耦合的waveport进行命名，比如1个waveport包含2个及以上的terminal的情况。

docs.title: 工具说明
docs.text:
Aedt Toolbox提供了一种便捷的方式，让用户在AEDT中迅速运行脚本或外部程序。它通过悬浮图标和自定义菜单的组合，实现高效操作。用户可以借助XML文件快速定制菜单内容，对脚本进行管理，一旦更新，这些变化将实时反映在右键菜单中，确保用户始终拥有个性化的操作体验。 整体实现效果如下：

当对应菜单被点击时，Toolbox会将脚本发送至最近一次打开的AEDT窗口并执行其内容。由于Toolbox不区分AEDT的版本，因此可以兼容不同版本的AEDT，使得同一个脚本可以在不同版本的AEDT中执行。

Toolbox支持外部程序和脚本的执行，包括Exe、Python和Ironpython三种类型。目前Toolbox仅支持Windows系统。

docs.title: 工具启动
docs.text:
通过目录下的"AedtToolbox.exe"启动Toolbox，启动后Toolbox以悬浮窗口的形式显示，默认显示在所有窗体的最前面。

可以通过两种方式弹出右键菜单： 1. 通过在悬浮窗口点击右键 1. 通过通知图标点击右键。

docs.title: 保持窗口最前
docs.text:
功能： 控制Toolbox悬浮窗口是否始终显示在其他窗口之上

  • 启用 - 勾选此选项后，Toolbox窗口将始终位于所有窗口顶层，不会被其他应用遮挡
  • 禁用 - 取消勾选后，Toolbox窗口可被其他窗口覆盖

特性： - 状态实时生效，无需重启工具 - 仅在当前会话有效，重启Toolbox后恢复为默认设置（由toolbox.cfg配置决定）

docs.title: 保持log窗口
docs.text:
功能： 控制Python/IronPython脚本执行时，是否保持命令行窗口打开

  • 启用 - 脚本执行后，命令行窗口（cmd）将保持打开状态，用户可查看脚本输出和调试信息 执行方式：cmd /K python entry.py ...（/K 表示执行后保持窗口）
  • 禁用 - 脚本执行后，命令行窗口立即关闭，仅当脚本出错时才能捕获错误信息 执行方式：python entry.py ...（直接执行，完成后关闭）

特性： - 状态持久化保存至 toolbox.cfg，重启工具后仍保持该设置 - 不影响EXE类型的外部程序执行 - 仅对 Python 和 IronPython 脚本有效

使用场景： - 开发调试阶段 - 启用此选项，观察脚本输出和错误 - 生产环境 - 禁用此选项，脚本执行完成后自动清理窗口

docs.title: Python环境配置
docs.text: Toolbox采用自动检测机制，按优先级依次寻找Python和IronPython环境。

docs.title: Python路径检测顺序（优先级从高到低）
docs.text:
1. 自定义PyAEDT环境 - %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts （用户自定义虚拟环境，优先级最高）
  2. 系统PATH环境变量 - 搜索PATH中的python.exe（用户已安装的Python）
  3. AEDT内置CPython - ANSYSEM_ROOTxxx\common\commonfiles\CPython\3_10\winx64\Release\python（AEDT自带，备选方案）
  4. IronPython - ANSYSEM_ROOTxxx\common\IronPython （用于IronPython脚本，始终加入PATH）

docs.title: pyaedt库安装
docs.text:
对于Python脚本的执行，pyaedt库是必需的。可通过以下命令安装：

pip install pyaedt

也可以使用提供的Toolkit进行安装，Toolkits->InstallPyaedtPannel

docs.title: 推荐配置方案
docs.text:
方案A：使用PyAEDT虚拟环境（推荐） - 在 %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts 建立虚拟环境 - 安装 pyaedt 及其依赖 - 优先级最高，不受系统Python变化影响

方案B：使用系统Python - 确保系统PATH中存在python.exe - 全局安装 pip install pyaedt - 适合已有Python开发环境的用户

方案C：使用AEDT内置Python - 若未配置前两种方案，自动使用AEDT自带的CPython - 需在AEDT对应版本中安装pyaedt

docs.title: 用户自定义菜单和脚本
用户子定义菜单和脚本
docs.text: 用户可以根据自身的需要添加菜单和脚本，定制符合本公司需求的Toolbox工具集合。可以参照下面的方法对菜单和脚本内容继续增加和删除。

docs.title: 菜单存放位置
docs.text:
Toolbox的菜单文件存放在 Menu/ 目录下，采用XML格式定义。

目录结构：

Toolbox安装目录/
├── AedtToolbox.exe
├── Menu/
│   ├── Default.xml           （基础菜单模板，全局适用）
│   ├── CircuitDesign.xml     （电路设计专用菜单）
│   ├── HFSS.xml              （HFSS设计专用菜单）
│   ├── HFSS-IE.xml           （HFSS-IE设计专用菜单）
│   ├── HFSSIE.xml            （HFSS IE设计专用菜单）
│   ├── Maxwell2D.xml         （Maxwell 2D设计专用菜单）
│   ├── Maxwell3D.xml         （Maxwell 3D设计专用菜单）
│   ├── HFSS3DLayoutDesign.xml（3D Layout设计专用菜单）
│   ├── Icepak.xml            （Icepak设计专用菜单）
│   ├── Q2DExtractor.xml      （Q2D提取器设计专用菜单）
│   ├── Q3DExtractor.xml      （Q3D提取器设计专用菜单）
│   ├── TwinBuilder.xml       （TwinBuilder设计专用菜单）
│   └── EMIT.xml              （EMIT设计专用菜单）
└── ...

菜单文件说明：

文件名 用途 优先级 Default.xml 所有设计类型的通用菜单，定义菜单的标准顺序和结构 基础 {DesignType}.xml 特定设计类型的专用菜单（如HFSS.xml、Maxwell3D.xml） 优先

docs.title: 菜单加载逻辑
docs.text: Toolbox采用智能菜单合并机制，根据当前活动的AEDT设计类型，动态加载并合并菜单。

docs.title: 菜单加载流程
docs.text:
用户右键点击 Toolbox
    ↓
contextMenuStrip1_Opening 事件触发
    ↓
获取菜单路径
    ├─ 检测当前活动AEDT窗口
    ├─ 查询活动设计的设计类型
    ├─ 生成菜单文件名 (e.g., Menu/HFSS.xml)
    └─ 检查菜单文件是否存在
    ↓
加载菜单
    ├─ 加载 {DesignType}.xml (e.g., Menu/HFSS.xml)
    ├─ 加载 Menu/Default.xml
    └─ 调用 MergeXmlNodes() 合并菜单
    ↓
菜单合并逻辑
    ├─ 以 {DesignType}.xml 的顺序为基准
    ├─ 从 Default.xml 中补充缺失的菜单项
    ├─ 保持 Default.xml 中各项的相对位置
    └─ 递归合并子菜单
    ↓
生成UI菜单项
    ├─ 创建 ToolStripMenuItem 和 ToolStripSeparator
    └─ 绑定菜单点击事件 Menu_Click
    ↓
显示菜单到用户

docs.title: 关键特性
docs.text:
1. 设计类型识别 - Toolbox自动检测当前活动的AEDT设计类型 - 支持的设计类型：CircuitDesign、HFSS、Maxwell3D、Icepak、Q3DExtractor等 - 若设计类型无专用菜单，自动降级使用Default.xml

2. 菜单合并规则 - 顺序基准 - {DesignType}.xml 决定菜单项的显示顺序 - 内容优先 - {DesignType}.xml 中的菜单定义优先级高于Default.xml - 自动补充 - Default.xml 中的项目自动补充到 {DesignType}.xml 中缺失的位置 - 相对位置保持 - 补充的项目保持在Default.xml中的相对位置

示例：

Default.xml 顺序：Help → SyncServer → About → Exit
HFSS.xml 顺序：  Tool → Toolkits → Exit

合并结果：  Tool → Toolkits → Help → SyncServer → About → Exit
          ↑      ↑          ↑      ↑           ↑       ↑
        HFSS   HFSS      Default  Default     Default Default

3. 实时更新 - 编辑任何Menu目录下的XML文件后，Toolbox会在下次打开菜单时自动重新加载 - 无需重启Toolbox即可生效

docs.title: 菜单的设定更新
docs.text:
用户菜单可以通过目录下的menu.xml进行更新和设定。Toolbox发布时已经附带了一些功能和菜单项，用户可以根据需要进行删减和添加自己的菜单内容。

菜单格式如下：

<Menu>
    <SubMenu Name="test">
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="showProjectName_python" ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Template/showProjectName.py" Arguments ="" PythonPath="" LogWindow="True"></SubMenu>
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="showProjectName_Ironpython" ExecuteType="IronPython" Path="$UserLib/Template/showProjectName.py" Arguments ="" EntryFunc="main" PythonPath=""></SubMenu>
    </SubMenu>
</Menu>

docs.title: 参数说明
docs.text:
• 时对菜单项的声明，允许进行嵌套，嵌套将以子菜单的形式呈现。子菜单的深度未作限定，但是不建议嵌套的过深，影响用户体验。
  • Type：取值可以为 "MenuItem"（菜单项）,"Separator"(分隔符), 省略时默认为"MenuItem"
  • Name：菜单显示的名称
  • ExecuteType：可执行程序的类型，可选值：Python，IronPython，EXE。 其中Command类型为内部保留类型，用户无法进行定义。
  • Path: 可执行程序，脚本的路径。可以使用绝对路径值。如果使用相对路径，可以使用"$+目录"的形式引入当面目录下的文件夹。
  • Arguments: 允许传递参数给可执行脚本，多个参数以空格隔开。
  • PythonPath： 可以指定Python的执行路径，特别是存在多个版本时可以按照路径区分版本。省略时会从Path变量中查找Python执行文件。
  • EntryFunc： 针对Python，IronPython指定运行脚本的入口函数，默认为"main"函数（可以省略），如果为其它函数则需要指定函数名称。
  • LogWindow: 运行脚本时是否显示运行日志，默认为True 显示日志窗口（命令行输出）， 设置为False 则不显示日志窗口。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
• 设计到文件路径的位置，空格和中文字符可能会导致执行错误，请尽量避免使用。
  • XML里面的文件路径注意使用\或者使用/。
  • XML的语法可以自行搜寻，修改后的温度不能存在语法错误。
  • 已知XML注释会导致菜单加载错误，请不用在XML文档中使用注释。
  • XML文件编辑保持后，Toolbox会自动重新加载。
  • LogWindow: 运行脚本时是否显示运行日志，默认为True 显示日志窗口（命令行输出）， 设置为False 则不显示日志窗口。

docs.title: Exe文件的添加
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="Notepad" ExecuteType="EXE" Path="Notepad.exe" Arguments =""/>

属性定义如下：

  • Name：菜单显示的名称
  • ExecuteType： "EXE"
  • Path：可执行文件路径。如果这里指定文档，且系统有默认执行程序，也可以顺利打开，比如Path指定xxx.docx文档。
  • Arguments: 传递参数，多个参数以空格隔开。
  • LogWindow: 运行脚本时是否显示运行日志，默认为True 显示日志窗口（命令行输出）， 设置为False 则不显示日志窗口。

docs.title: Python脚本的添加
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt" Arguments ="" PythonPath=""></SubMenu>

属性定义如下：

  • Name：菜单显示的名称
  • ExecuteType：Python
  • Path: 脚本的路径。可以使用绝对路径值。如果使用相对路径，可以使用"$+目录"的形式引入当面目录下的文件夹。
  • Arguments: 可选，允许传递参数给可执行脚本，多个参数以空格隔开。
  • PythonPath： 可选，可以指定Python的执行路径，特别是存在多个版本时可以按照路径区分版本。省略时会从Path变量中查找Python执行文件。
  • EntryFunc： 可选，针对Python，IronPython指定运行脚本的入口函数，默认为"main"函数（可以省略），如果为其它函数则需要指定函数名称。
  • LogWindow: 运行脚本时是否显示运行日志，默认为True 显示日志窗口（命令行输出）， 设置为False 则不显示日志窗口。

docs.title: IronPython脚本的添加
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt" Arguments ="" PythonPath=""></SubMenu>

属性定义如下：

  • Name：菜单显示的名称
  • ExecuteType：Python
  • Path: 脚本的路径。可以使用绝对路径值。如果使用相对路径，可以使用"$+目录"的形式引入当面目录下的文件夹。
  • Arguments: 可选，允许传递参数给可执行脚本，多个参数以空格隔开。
  • PythonPath： 可选，可以指定Python的执行路径，特别是存在多个版本时可以按照路径区分版本。省略时会从Path变量中查找Python执行文件。
  • EntryFunc： 可选，针对Python，IronPython指定运行脚本的入口函数，默认为"main"函数（可以省略），如果为其它函数则需要指定函数名称。
  • LogWindow: 运行脚本时是否显示运行日志，默认为True 显示日志窗口（命令行输出）， 设置为False 则不显示日志窗口。

docs.title: 功能描述
docs.text:
• Close->QuitAedt ：强制关闭aedt
  • Close->CloseProjectAndSave ：关闭当前工程并保存
  • Close->CloseProjectNotSave ：关闭当前工程不保存
  • Close->CloseAllProjectAndSave ：关闭所有工程并保存
  • Close->CloseAllProjectNotSave ：关闭所有工程不保存
  • Close->ReloadProject ： 重新加载工程

docs.title: 添加UDM/UDP脚本
docs.text:
UDM,UDP可以实现更加复杂的模型构建，可以在HFSS, Icepak, Maxwell3D, 和Q3D中使用。 工具支持将UDM和UDP脚本添加到菜单中，具体方法如下： 

    <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="UDPTest" ExecuteType="Python" Path="xxxx/geodesic_sphere.py" EntryFunc="$UDP" LogWindow="False"></SubMenu>
    <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="UDMTest" ExecuteType="Python" Path="xxxx/OnDieSpiralInductor.py" EntryFunc="$UDM" LogWindow="False"></SubMenu>

docs.title: 参数说明
docs.text:
等EntryFunc设定为$UDM，工具会认为执行的脚本为UDM脚本，工具会将脚本发送到对应的AEDT窗口，执行UDM命令。 等EntryFunc设定为$UDP，工具会认为执行的脚本为UDP脚本，工具会将脚本发送到对应的AEDT窗口，执行UDP命令。 发布时建议LogWindow设置为"False"，避免弹出日志窗口。 其它参数请参考[Write your Script]中的说明。

UDM和UDP脚本的编写方法请参考Help说明，按照正常的步骤编写脚本，并保存为.py文件即可，脚本可以放置在任意位置，如果放置在工具目录可以使用先对路径。 UDM和UDP的执行效果和软件点击菜单执行效果一致，会弹出设置窗口。。

  1. 在右键菜单中选择对应的UDP或者UDM脚本

  2. 弹出UDP/UDM设置窗口

Note: 工具会在UDM/UDP下面自动添加一个UDMScript/UDPScript菜单项作为入口，如果产出会重新产生。 （如果用户在sysLib目录没有权限，可以尝试将DMScript/UDPScript手动添加到UserLib目录下）

docs.title: python脚本的编写
docs.text:
python脚本可以按照正常的模式进行开发，将需要运行到功能包装成一个函数，最终在菜单的xml里面指定未入口函数即可。 默认的入口函数未mian()函数，建议使用main()函数作为入口。

下面的案例中，python文件定义了多个函数，可以在xml里面分别指定不同的函数作为入口函数，完成不同的功能。 另外也可以通过传递不同的Arguments来完成类似的功能。


import sys,os

Module = sys.modules['__main__']
if hasattr(Module, "oDesktop"):
    oDesktop = getattr(Module, "oDesktop")
else:
    raise Exception("oDesktop intial error.")


def closeAndSave():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    oProject.Save()
    oProject.Close() 

def closeNotSave():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    # oProject.Save()
    oProject.Close() #Unsaved changes will be lost.

def closeAllProjectWithSave():
    projects = oDesktop.GetProjects()
    for oProject in projects:
        oProject.Save()
        oProject.Close()

def closeAllProjectWithoutSave():
    projects = oDesktop.GetProjects()
    for oProject in projects:
        # oProject.Save()
        oProject.Close() #Unsaved changes will be lost.

def ForceQuitAedt():
    oDesktop.QuitApplication()

def Reload():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    aedtPath = os.path.join(oProject.GetPath(),oProject.GetName()+".aedt")
    oProject.Close()
    print("Reload aedt:%s"%aedtPath)
    oDesktop.OpenProject(aedtPath)

XML菜单配置

    <SubMenu Name="Close">
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt"></SubMenu>
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="CloseProjectAndSave" ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="closeAndSave"></SubMenu>

docs.title: pyaedt下载离线包
docs.text: 工具的运行需要安装pyaedt，如果受限于网络不能通过pip安装，可以从下面的连接下载对应python版本的离线版本： https://github.com/ansys/pyaedt

docs.title: 联系作者
docs.text: yongsheng.guo@synopsys.com

docs.title: 工具概述
docs.text: addPyaedtPanels 是一个标准化的PyAEDT面板安装工具，提供图形用户界面(GUI)来简化在Windows环境下的PyAEDT配置和集成流程。该工具支持从多种来源安装PyAEDT，并自动将其集成到AEDT设计环境中。

docs.title: 系统需求
docs.text:
• 操作系统: Windows
  • Python版本: 3.10 或更高版本
  • AEDT版本: 2023.2 或更高版本
  • 网络环境: 支持在线和离线(wheel文件)安装

docs.title: 工具位置
docs.text:
PyAEDT面板安装工具位于Toolbox安装目录下：

Toolbox安装目录/
├── UserLib/
│   └── Toolkits/
│       └── addPyaedtPanels/
│           ├── install_pyaedt_gui.py       （主程序 - GUI应用）
│           ├── refactor_utils.py           （共享工具函数）
│           └── 使用说明-郭永生-*.pdf      （使用文档）
└── ...

docs.title: 1. 虚拟环境管理 (create_venv)
docs.text:
• 功能: 创建专用的Python 3.10虚拟环境
  • 存储位置: %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\ (Windows)
  • 特点:
  • 自动检测既有环境，避免重复创建
  • 隔离的依赖管理，不影响系统Python
  • 支持AEDT内置Python直接使用

docs.title: 方式一：在线安装 (install_pyaedt_online)
docs.text:
• 优点: 获取最新版本，自动解决依赖
  • 缺点: 需要网络连接
  • 命令: 通过pip从官方源安装

docs.title: 方式二：离线安装 (install_pyaedt_from_wheel)
docs.text:
• 优点: 无需网络，安装速度快
  • 缺点: 需要提前下载wheel文件
  • 文件格式: .whl 或 .zip (自动解压)
  • 使用场景: 企业内网环境，多机部署

docs.title: 3. AEDT面板集成 (add_pyaedt_to_aedt)
docs.text:
• 功能: 将PyAEDT注册为AEDT设计面板
  • 效果: 安装后可在AEDT中直接使用PyAEDT API
  • 自动化: 调用PyAEDT官方安装脚本完成集成

docs.title: 整体执行流程
docs.text:
启动 install_pyaedt_gui.py
    ↓
检测AEDT环境
    ├─ 若在AEDT内执行
    │  └─ 使用AEDT自带Python (v261+ CPython 3.10)重新启动
    └─ 若独立执行
       └─ 使用系统Python 3.10+继续
    ↓
打开图形界面
    ├─ 方式选择 (在线/离线)
    ├─ 虚拟环境管理
    ├─ 安装进度监控
    └─ 结果反馈
    ↓
虚拟环境创建
    └─ 于 %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\ 创建venv
    ↓
PyAEDT安装
    ├─ 在线: pip install pyaedt[all]
    └─ 离线: pip install from wheel --find-links=<path>
    ↓
AEDT面板集成
    └─ 调用 ansys.aedt.core.extensions.installer.pyaedt_installer
    ↓
安装完成
    └─ 提示用户重启AEDT以应用更改

docs.title: AEDT根目录检测
docs.text:
工具自动扫描环境变量查找AEDT安装路径：

# 支持的环境变量格式 (大小写不敏感)
AnsysEM_ROOT241    # AEDT 2024.1
ANSYSEM_ROOT232    # AEDT 2023.2
ansysEM_root261    # AEDT 2026.1 (也支持)

docs.title: 搜索策略
docs.text:
1. 检查所有形式的 {AnsysEM_ROOT|ANSYSEM_ROOT|ansysem_root}+{3位版本号} 环境变量
  2. 验证对应路径的有效性
  3. 按版本号降序排列(最新版本优先)

示例: - 若环境中同时存在 ANSYSEM_ROOT232 和 ANSYSEM_ROOT241，将选择 241 作为主版本

docs.title: 启动方式
docs.text:
方式1: 直接在Toolbox菜单中调用 - 右键Toolbox → 选择"PyAEDT Panel Installer" - 自动检测AEDT环境并启动GUI

方式2: 命令行启动

python install_pyaedt_gui.py

方式3: 在AEDT IronPython控制台执行

exec(open(r"C:\...\addPyaedtPanels\install_pyaedt_gui.py").read())

docs.title: 界面操作步骤
docs.text:
1. 环境检测 - 程序自动检测AEDT版本和Python解释器
  2. 安装方式选择 - 选择"在线安装"或"离线安装"
  3. 虚拟环境 - 自动创建或使用既有环境
  4. 安装配置 - 设置安装选项(如依赖包)
  5. 进度监控 - 实时显示安装进度
  6. 完成反馈 - 成功/失败提示和日志

docs.title: 多线程处理
docs.text:
• UI线程: 保持界面响应性
  • 工作线程: 后台执行安装任务
  • 队列通信: 使用Queue进行线程间消息传递
  • 超时控制: 长时间操作自动超时预警

docs.title: 常见问题
docs.text: 问题 原因 解决方案 "AEDT Python not found" AEDT版本过旧 升级AEDT到2023.2+ "权限拒绝" 虚拟环境路径无写权限 修改%APPDATA%权限或更换安装路径 安装超时 网络不稳定或源服务器缓慢 使用离线wheel安装或重试 PersonalLib路径不存在 AEDT尚未创建默认目录 程序自动创建，无需手动处理

docs.title: 日志查看
docs.text:
• GUI日志: 安装界面中的"日志"标签页
  • 系统日志: %TEMP%\configure_pyaedt.py 执行结果
  • 错误追踪: 检查venv中的pip安装日志

docs.title: Python虚拟环境自动识别
docs.text:
安装完成后，虚拟环境自动被Toolbox的Python检测机制识别：

Toolbox启动时执行InitPythonEnv()
    ↓
检测 %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts\python.exe
    ↓
若存在 → 作为优先使用的Python
    ↓
后续所有Python脚本使用此环境执行

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
⚠️ 重要提示

  1. 不修改生成文件: install_pyaedt_gui.py 是从标准模板生成，不应手动修改
  2. AEDT重启: 安装PyAEDT panel后需要重启AEDT才能加载新面板
  3. 权限要求: 需要对%APPDATA%目录的写入权限
  4. 版本兼容性: 虚拟环境路径与AEDT版本无关，可跨版本重用
  5. 卸载说明: 删除 %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\ 目录即可完全卸载

docs.title: Q2D 参数化传输线生成工具 (q2d_TlineGenerator)
工具概述
docs.text:
q2d_TlineGenerator 是一个面向 Ansys Q2D Extractor 的快速建模工具，用于自动生成参数化多线传输线截面。工具通过一个简洁的 GUI 让用户输入线条数量，然后在 AEDT 中自动创建新的 2D Extractor 设计，并完成导体、介质、材料、粗糙度模型和求解设置的初始化。

该工具适合以下场景：

  • 快速搭建多根并行传输线的 Q2D 截面模型
  • 为后续阻抗、耦合、RLGC 参数分析准备统一模板
  • 批量开展线宽、线距、介质参数的参数扫描

docs.title: 系统需求
docs.text:
• 运行环境: Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT)
  • 设计类型: Q2D Extractor
  • 脚本方式: Python 菜单脚本
  • 界面依赖: Tkinter GUI

docs.title: 工具位置
docs.text:
工具位于 Toolbox 安装目录下：

Toolbox安装目录/
├── UserLib/
│   └── Toolkits/
│       └── q2d_TlineGenerator/
│           └── TlineGenerator.py    （主程序）
└── ...

docs.title: 菜单配置
docs.text:
该工具对应的菜单项配置如下：

<SubMenu Type="MenuItem"
         Name="q2d_TlineGenerator"
         ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib\toolkits\q2d_TlineGenerator\TlineGenerator.py" />

docs.title: 界面说明
docs.text:
工具启动后会弹出一个简单输入窗口，包含以下内容：

  • Line Count: 需要生成的传输线数量
  • OK: 开始创建模型
  • Cancel: 关闭窗口，不执行任何建模操作

docs.title: 输入规则
docs.text:
• 仅接受正整数
  • 默认值为 4
  • 若输入为空、非整数或小于等于 0，程序会弹出错误提示

docs.title: 自动生成内容
docs.text: 点击 OK 后，工具会在当前 AEDT 工程中自动插入一个新的 2D Extractor 设计，并完成以下内容。

docs.title: 1. 参数变量
docs.text: 工具会按线条数量自动创建一组局部变量，便于后续在 AEDT 中继续调参。

docs.title: 线宽与间距
docs.text:
• w_line1_top ~ w_lineN_top: 每根线顶部宽度
  • w_line1_bot ~ w_lineN_bot: 每根线底部宽度
  • s_line1_line2 ~ s_line(N-1)_lineN: 相邻线之间的间距
  • s_left, s_right: 左右边界留白

docs.title: 厚度与介质参数
docs.text:
• T_gnd_top, T_gnd_bot: 上下参考地厚度
  • T_line: 线条厚度
  • H_core: 底部芯板厚度
  • H_pp: 顶部 PP 介质厚度

docs.title: 自动定位变量
docs.text:
工具会继续生成以下位置变量，用于自动排布多根线：

  • line1_left, line1_right
  • line2_left, line2_right
  • ...
  • lineN_left, lineN_right
  • max_right: 整个截面的总宽度上边界

docs.title: 2. 材料参数
docs.text:
工具会创建一组可在工程级别统一修改的材料变量：

  • $pp_dk, $core_dk, $fill_dk
  • $pp_df, $core_df, $fill_df
  • $cond_line, $cond_top_gnd, $cond_bot_gnd

默认值分别对应常见 PCB 介质和铜导体初始参数，例如：

  • 介电常数默认值: 3.6
  • 损耗角正切默认值: 0.008
  • 电导率默认值: 5.8e7

docs.title: 3. 几何对象
docs.text:
工具会在 3D Modeler 中自动创建以下对象：

  • tline1 ~ tlineN: 多根参数化传输线导体
  • gnd_bot: 底部参考地
  • gnd_top: 顶部参考地
  • core: 芯板介质
  • fill: 走线所在填充介质
  • pp: 上方 PP 介质

其中：

  • 传输线采用梯形截面建模，顶部宽度与底部宽度可独立设置
  • 上下地平面宽度自动跟随 max_right
  • 介质区域与导体区域位置由变量驱动，便于后续优化

docs.title: 4. 粗糙度模型
docs.text: 工具默认给导体和地平面分配 Huray surface roughness 参数。

docs.title: 传输线粗糙度变量
docs.text:
• huray_radius_line_left
  • huray_radius_line_top
  • huray_radius_line_right
  • huray_radius_line_bot
  • huray_ratio_line_left
  • huray_ratio_line_top
  • huray_ratio_line_right
  • huray_ratio_line_bot

docs.title: 地平面粗糙度变量
docs.text:
• huray_radius_gnd_top
  • huray_radius_gnd_bot
  • huray_ratio_gnd_top
  • huray_ratio_gnd_bot

默认半径为 0.5um，默认比例为 2.9。

docs.title: 5. 边界与求解设置
docs.text:
工具会自动完成以下 Q2D 设置：

  • 将 gnd_bot 和 gnd_top 指定为参考地
  • 将每根 tlineX 指定为独立信号线
  • 创建 Setup1 分析设置
  • 设置自适应频率为 10GHz
  • 自动插入 Sweep1 频率扫描

docs.title: 默认扫描范围
docs.text:
频率扫描分为两段对数采样：

  • 1Hz 到 1GHz
  • 1GHz 到 100GHz

docs.title: 执行流程
docs.text:
启动 q2d_TlineGenerator
    ↓
弹出参数输入窗口
    ↓
输入 Line Count
    ↓
新建 2D Extractor 设计
    ↓
创建局部变量与工程变量
    ↓
创建介质材料与导体材料
    ↓
生成多根传输线、上下地与介质层
    ↓
分配信号线/参考地边界
    ↓
设置 Huray 粗糙度模型
    ↓
插入 Setup1 与 Sweep1
    ↓
完成参数化模型初始化

docs.title: 操作步骤
docs.text:
1. 在 AEDT 中打开目标工程
  2. 通过 Toolbox 菜单启动 q2d_TlineGenerator
  3. 输入需要的线条数量，例如 4、8 或 16
  4. 点击 OK 生成初始模型
  5. 在新建的 Q2D 设计中修改线宽、线距、介质厚度和材料参数
  6. 根据分析目标运行仿真或建立 Optimetrics 参数扫描

docs.title: 适合后续调节的参数
docs.text:
生成完成后，通常建议优先修改以下变量：

  • w_line*_top, w_line*_bot
  • s_line*_line*
  • T_line
  • H_core, H_pp
  • $core_dk, $pp_dk, $fill_dk

docs.title: 并行总线截面建模
docs.text: 当需要评估多根并行走线的耦合、特征阻抗和损耗时，可先用该工具快速生成标准化截面，再逐步调整各线宽度与间距。

docs.title: 工艺窗口研究
docs.text: 由于顶部和底部线宽是独立变量，该工具也适合模拟蚀刻后形成的梯形截面，用于比较不同工艺偏差对阻抗与串扰的影响。

docs.title: 粗糙度敏感性分析
docs.text: 通过修改 Huray 相关变量，可以快速研究表面粗糙度对高频损耗和 RLGC 参数的影响。

docs.title: 注意事项
docs.text:
1. 工具每次执行都会在当前工程中插入一个新的 2D Extractor 设计
  2. 该工具负责建立参数化模型，不会自动启动求解
  3. 若当前环境中没有可用的 AEDT 上下文，脚本会尝试通过外部接口初始化桌面会话
  4. 线条数量越大，后续边界、网格与求解规模也会明显增加
  5. 创建完成后建议先检查变量默认值是否符合当前板材和铜厚定义

docs.title: 故障排查
docs.text: 问题 可能原因 处理建议 启动后无模型生成 未连接有效 AEDT 会话 确认从 Toolbox/AEDT 环境中启动脚本 提示输入非法 Line Count 不是正整数 输入 1 以上整数 设计创建成功但尺寸异常 默认变量不符合当前设计需求 检查并修改 w_line*、s_left/right、H_core 等变量 高频损耗偏大或偏小 粗糙度参数不符合真实工艺 调整 Huray 半径与比例参数

docs.text: Welcome to AedtToolbox — a floating toolbar for Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT) that provides rapid script execution and tool integration.

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Via → AutoBackdrill

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Automatically sets up via backdrill based on net names and stub length. Supports AEDT 2023 R1 and later. Automatically determines the backdrill direction.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
Ensure the 3D Layout project is selected, then right-click Toolbox and select Via → AutoBackdrill. The AutoBackdrill settings dialog will appear.

  • Net Match (Regex) — Use a regular expression to match nets for backdrill. Default .* matches all nets.
  • Backdrill Stub — Backdrill stub length accuracy; default is 8 mil.

docs.title: Net Match (Regex)
docs.text:
• Use .* to match any number of characters. For example, DQ.* matches all nets starting with DQ.
  • Regex syntax reference: https://www.runoob.com/regexp/regexp-syntax.html

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Bus notation is also supported. For example, DQ[15:0] matches nets DQ0 through DQ15. (Note: this bus notation is not standard regex — it is an extension supported by this tool.)

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Net → AutoXNet

docs.title: Description
docs.text: When a signal trace passes through a capacitor and one side of the net is unnamed (numeric or random name), this script automatically names the unnamed side based on the already-named side. New net names are suffixed with _C, _R, or _L depending on component type (currently defaults to capacitors only).

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: Ensure the 3D Layout project is selected, then right-click Toolbox and select Net → AutoXNet. The script runs automatically in the active AEDT session, detecting capacitor nets and naming any unnamed ones.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Power/GND nets defined in 3D Layout are automatically skipped.
  • Capacitors must be assigned to the Capacitors category in the Component list.

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Export → ExportToHfssWithNets

docs.title: Description
docs.text: After exporting a 3D model from 3D Layout to HFSS, this script groups objects by Net, making it easier to browse and configure them.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: Ensure the 3D Layout project is selected and fully configured (stackup, ports, cutout, etc.), with an HFSS Setup defined (ideally only one). Then right-click Toolbox and select Export → ExportToHfssWithNets.

docs.title: Result
docs.text: After the script completes, the result looks like:

docs.title: Overlapping Stackup Support
docs.text: For 2.5D/3DIC packages using Overlapping stackup in 3D Layout, ExportToHfssWithNets supports the export. However, if two or more metal layers share the same height, Net recognition may have issues.

docs.title: Interposer Export Example:
docs.text:
1. Overlapping stackup with all metal layers at different heights
  2. Before exporting, the Design Mode must be General Mode, otherwise the script will fail
  3. Export result

docs.title: Note
docs.text: 3D Layout using the HFSS solver achieves the same accuracy as HFSS 3D, but is faster and easier to set up. In most cases, exporting to HFSS 3D is not recommended.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
HfssAutoSetupGUI is a GUI-based configuration selector for the AEDT 3D Layout / HFSS Layout workflow. It reads CSV templates from the config folder next to the script, allows users to choose a configuration type and a preset, and then applies the corresponding HFSS setup and airbox settings to the current design.

The purpose of this tool is not manual setup entry. It standardizes commonly used simulation presets so repeated HFSS setup work can be reduced and team-wide settings can remain consistent.

docs.title: Typical Use Cases
docs.text:
• Quickly apply standard HFSS setups for SerDes and DDRx channels
  • Reuse common sweep ranges, convergence settings, and airbox sizes
  • Reduce manual setup errors and configuration drift between projects

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text:
3DLayout -> Setup -> HfssAutoSetupGUI

The corresponding menu item is:

<SubMenu Type="MenuItem"
         Name="HfssAutoSetupGUI"
         ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib/3DLayout/Setup/HfssAutoSetupGUI.py"
         Arguments=""
         PythonPath=""></SubMenu>

docs.title: Tool Location
docs.text:
Toolbox installation directory/
├── UserLib/
│   └── 3DLayout/
│       └── Setup/
│           ├── HfssAutoSetupGUI.py   (GUI entry script)
│           ├── autoSetup.py          (setup execution logic)
│           └── config/
│               ├── DDRx.csv
│               └── Serdes.csv
└── ...

docs.title: Interface
docs.text:
When launched, the tool opens a Windows Forms dialog with the following fields:

  • Select Type: Chooses the configuration category, mapped to CSV filenames in the config folder
  • Select Config: Chooses a preset inside the selected CSV file, mapped to the header row
  • Config Preview: Displays the key-value pairs of the selected preset
  • OK: Applies the selected setup to the active design
  • Cancel: Closes the dialog without making changes

docs.title: Detected Configuration Types
docs.text:
Based on the current script directory, the tool loads these configuration groups by default:

  • Serdes
  • DDRx

If more CSV files are added to the config directory later, they will automatically appear in the Select Type dropdown without GUI code changes.

docs.title: Input Source
docs.text:
This tool does not require the user to type simulation parameters manually. All setup values come from CSV-based templates.

The CSV format is organized as follows:

  • The first row defines configuration names
  • The first column defines parameter keys
  • The remaining cells define the value for each configuration

Example keys include:

  • Setup/Name
  • SolutionType
  • AdaptiveFrequency
  • Order
  • DeltaS
  • Setup/Sweep/Name
  • SweepType
  • InterpolatingTolerance
  • SweepData
  • UseQ3DForDC
  • Airbox/Options/AirHorExt/Ext

docs.title: Output Behavior
docs.text:
After OK is clicked, the tool converts the selected preset into a parameter dictionary and passes it to HfssAutoSetup(data).run().

The actual output behavior includes:

  • Initializing the active AEDT layout design context
  • Applying HFSS setup parameters
  • Applying sweep parameters
  • Applying airbox parameters
  • Closing the dialog after completion

docs.title: User Steps
docs.text:
1. Make sure the active project is a valid 3D Layout design for HFSS analysis
  2. Launch HfssAutoSetupGUI from the Toolbox menu
  3. Choose a category in Select Type, such as Serdes or DDRx
  4. Choose a preset in Select Config, such as a target data-rate profile
  5. Review the parameters in Config Preview
  6. Click OK to apply the setup

docs.title: Execution Flow
docs.text:
Launch HfssAutoSetupGUI
    ↓
Scan CSV files under the config folder
    ↓
Populate the Select Type dropdown
    ↓
Read the CSV header for the selected type
    ↓
Populate the Select Config dropdown
    ↓
Convert the selected column into a parameter dictionary
    ↓
Show the configuration in the preview area
    ↓
Click OK
    ↓
Call HfssAutoSetup(data)
    ↓
Initialize the current Layout design
    ↓
Run solveSetup()
    ↓
Run setAirbox()
    ↓
Finish automatic setup

docs.title: Serdes.csv
docs.text:
Serdes.csv provides a set of SerDes-oriented presets, for example:

  • SERDES_8G
  • SERDES_16G
  • SERDES_25G
  • SERDES_32G
  • SERDES_56G
  • SERDES_112G
  • SERDES_224G
  • SERDES_448G

These presets typically define:

  • Multiple adaptive frequencies
  • Interpolating sweeps
  • Linear sweep sampling for high-frequency analysis
  • Fixed horizontal and vertical airbox extensions

docs.title: DDRx.csv
docs.text:
DDRx.csv provides DDR-related presets, for example:

  • DDRx_3200
  • DDRx_6400
  • DDRx_10700
  • DDRx_17600

These presets commonly adjust:

  • Adaptive frequency
  • Sweep stop frequency
  • Basis order
  • Whether UseQ3DForDC is enabled

docs.title: Preview Area
docs.text:
The preview area displays the selected configuration as key : value pairs before execution.

Display rules:

  • The top line shows the current Type and Config
  • The remaining lines show all available keys in sorted order
  • Only non-empty values from the selected configuration column are displayed

This means a parameter will not appear in the preview or in the generated configuration dictionary if its cell is empty in the selected CSV column.

docs.title: Actual Execution Logic
docs.text:
HfssAutoSetupGUI.py mainly handles the interface and CSV parsing. The actual setup work is performed by the HfssAutoSetup class in autoSetup.py.

According to the current implementation, the effective run() steps are:

  1. Initialize Layout() and bind the current design
  2. Call solveSetup() to write the solution and sweep settings
  3. Call setAirbox() to write the airbox settings

The backend script also keeps several commented future or optional workflow steps, such as:

  • Cutout
  • Net configuration
  • Component model configuration
  • Backdrill
  • Pin group
  • Solder ball generation
  • Port creation
  • Source creation
  • Geometry check
  • Analyze

The current GUI version does not automatically execute those steps, and it does not automatically run the simulation.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. This tool depends on CSV files under the config directory. If the folder is missing or empty, no presets will be loaded
  2. The tool applies template-based settings and does not validate whether every parameter fully matches the current board structure
  3. The current implementation mainly automates HFSS setup and airbox configuration, not a full one-click simulation flow
  4. If a CSV key is not recognized by the backend config object, a key not found warning may be logged
  5. Before execution, verify that the active design already satisfies the basic conditions for HFSS 3D Layout analysis

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Cause Suggested Fix The type list is empty The config folder is missing or contains no CSV files Check UserLib/3DLayout/Setup/config/ No config appears after selecting a type The CSV header row is not formatted correctly Verify that configuration names start from the second column of the first row The preview is empty The selected preset column has no valid key-value pairs Check whether the selected CSV column is empty Clicking OK raises an error The active design context is invalid, or the keys are incompatible Run the tool inside the correct AEDT design and verify the CSV keys The applied setup is not as expected The preset does not match the project Review the preview first, then adjust the CSV template

docs.title: Related Resources
docs.text:
• HFSS 3D Layout documentation
  • Toolbox menu configuration guide: ToolBox/AddItem.md
  • Related tool example: HFSS3DLayout/AutoBackdrill.md

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Stackup → ImportCSVStackup

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Quickly import a stackup from a CSV file provided by the PCB manufacturer. Convert the manufacturer's xlsx file into the provided CSV template format. Column order is flexible; unrecognized columns are ignored.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
Fill in the stackup information using the provided CSV template. Then right-click Toolbox, select Stackup → ImportCSVStackup, and choose the completed CSV file to import.

Two templates are provided in the ImportCSVStackup.py directory: - CsvStackupTemp_full.csv - CsvStackupTemp_simple.csv

docs.title: Template Format
docs.text:
Layer Type Thickness(mil) Cond DK DF Roughness UNNAMED_000 dielectric 0 3.7 0.15 0.5um TOP signal 1.9 3.7 0.15 0.5um UNNAMED_002 dielectric 2.65 3.5 0.004 0.5um PWR signal 1.3 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_004 dielectric 50 3.5 0.004 0.5um LYR_1 signal 1.2 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_006 dielectric 8 3.5 0.004 0.5um LYR_2 signal 1.2 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_008 dielectric 8 3.5 0.004 0.5um GND signal 1.3 3.5 0.004 0.5um UNNAMED_010 dielectric 2.65 3.5 0.004 0.5um BOTTOM signal 1.9 3.7 0.15 0.5um UNNAMED_012 dielectric 0 3.7 0.15 0.5um

Notes: 1. Layer, Type, and Thickness are required; rows without them are ignored. 2. If Cond, DK, or DF are missing or empty, defaults of Copper and FR4_epoxy are used. 3. Thickness can specify units in parentheses, or include units in the value itself. 4. Roughness is optional. Groiss format: 0.5um; Huray format: 0.5um;2.9

docs.title: Optional Columns
docs.text:
• Thickness — Layer thickness with unit, or use a global unit in the header (e.g. Thickness(mil) or Thickness(mm))
  • Material — Material name from the system library (e.g. Copper); when specified, Cond/DK/DF are ignored

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The number of metal layers in the CSV must match the PCB; otherwise the import will fail.
  • The number of dielectric layers does not need to match — if they differ, the CSV dielectric layers will override those in the PCB.

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
1. Excel stackup file from the PCB manufacturer
  2. Export existing stackup template using ExportCSVStackup
  3. Import the modified CSV using ImportCSVStackup
  4. Log output

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Via → KeepHDIViaPad

docs.title: Description
docs.text: 3D Layout omits intermediate pads on HDI stacked vias by default, even though those pads physically exist. The KeepHDIViaPad script detects HDI through-holes and adds equal-size circular shapes on each layer to simulate the actual pads.

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Port → RenameEdgePort

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Renames manually added ports in 3D Layout for easier identification.

docs.title: Naming Rules
docs.text:
• If the port is on a Pin: named as refdes.pin.Net — e.g. U1.A2.DQ0
  • If the port is not on a Pin: named using the net name

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: After adding ports manually, keep the 3D Layout window in focus and run Port → RenameEdgePort. Ports are automatically updated.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: Renaming coupled wave ports (one wave port with 2 or more terminals) is not currently supported.

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Component → DissolveInvalidRLC (<2 pins) Component → deleteInvalidComponents (<2 pins)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Removes RLC components that have only one valid net connection. After performing a Cutout or deleting nets in a layout, some RLC components may have their nets removed and become invalid. This command clears those invalid RLC components.

  • DissolveInvalidRLC (<2 pins) — Only dissolves the RLC component; does not remove pads
  • deleteInvalidComponents (<2 pins) — Deletes all components with fewer than 2 pins (RLC and others) including their pads

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: Ensure the 3D Layout project is selected, then right-click Toolbox and select Component → deleteInvalidRLC. The script runs automatically in the active AEDT session, detects net status of RLC components, and removes invalid ones.

docs.title: Menu Location
docs.text: Port → orderPorts

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Sorts 3D Layout ports by Net using the following rules: 1. Component (refdes) 2. Bus index (numeric suffix on net name) 3. Differential pair P/N, +/−, P/M

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: Keep the 3D Layout window in focus and run Port → orderPorts.

docs.title: S-parameter output example
docs.text:
# GHz S MA R 50.000000
! Terminal data exported
! Port[1] = XS23.AE19.MCTR2_CNIP1CH2FP_GTX_T0_P
! Port[2] = XS23.AD19.MCTR2_CNIP1CH2FP_GTX_T0_N
! Port[3] = XS23.AE21.MCTR2_CNIP1CH2FP_GTX_T1_P
! Port[4] = XS23.AD21.MCTR2_CNIP1CH2FP_GTX_T1_N
! Port[5] = XS23.AE23.MCTR2_CNIP1CH2FP_GTX_T2_P

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
AedtToolbox provides a convenient way to quickly run scripts or external programs within AEDT. It combines a floating icon with a customizable menu for efficient operation. Users can rapidly customize menu contents via XML files to manage scripts, and any changes are immediately reflected in the right-click menu, ensuring a personalized experience.

Overall effect:

When a menu item is clicked, Toolbox sends the script to the most recently opened AEDT window and executes it. Since Toolbox is version-agnostic, the same script can run across different AEDT versions.

Toolbox supports execution of external programs and scripts including: EXE, Python, and IronPython. Currently only Windows is supported.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Launch Toolbox by running AedtToolbox.exe. After startup, Toolbox displays as a floating window on top of all other windows by default.

Two ways to open the right-click menu: 1. Right-click on the floating window 2. Right-click the notification tray icon

docs.title: Keep on Top
docs.text:
Function: Controls whether the Toolbox window stays above all other windows.

  • Enabled — Toolbox window stays on top of all applications at all times
  • Disabled — Toolbox window can be covered by other windows

Characteristics: - Takes effect immediately without restarting - Session-scoped only; resets to default on restart (controlled by toolbox.cfg)

docs.title: Keep Log Window
docs.text:
Function: Controls whether the command window stays open after Python/IronPython script execution.

  • Enabled — The command prompt (cmd) stays open after execution for viewing output and debugging Execution: cmd /K python entry.py ... (/K keeps window open)
  • Disabled — The command window closes immediately after execution Execution: python entry.py ... (closes when done)

Characteristics: - State is persisted to toolbox.cfg and survives restarts - Does not affect EXE-type external programs - Applies to Python and IronPython scripts only

Use Cases: - Development/Debugging — Enable to see script output and errors - Production — Disable for a clean execution experience

docs.title: Python Environment Configuration
docs.text: Toolbox uses automatic detection to find Python and IronPython in priority order.

docs.title: Python Detection Order (highest to lowest priority)
docs.text:
1. Custom PyAEDT venv — %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts (user-defined virtual environment)
  2. System PATH — Searches PATH for python.exe (user-installed Python)
  3. AEDT bundled CPython — ANSYSEM_ROOTxxx\common\commonfiles\CPython\3_10\winx64\Release\python
  4. IronPython — ANSYSEM_ROOTxxx\common\IronPython (always added to PATH for IronPython scripts)

docs.title: Installing the pyaedt Library
docs.text:
For Python scripts, pyaedt is required. Install with:

pip install pyaedt

Or use the provided Toolkit: Toolkits → InstallPyaedtPannel

docs.title: Recommended Configuration
docs.text:
Option A: Use PyAEDT Virtual Environment (Recommended) - Create a venv at %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts - Install pyaedt and dependencies - Highest priority, unaffected by system Python changes

Option B: Use System Python - Install Python 3.10+ and add to system PATH - Run: pip install pyaedt - Simple setup, suitable for single-user environments

Option C: Use AEDT Bundled Python - No setup required if AEDT >= 2023.2 is installed - Lower priority; used as fallback

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Users can add menus and scripts to build a customized Toolbox suited to their company's needs. Use the methods below to add or remove menu items and scripts.

docs.title: Menu Storage Location
docs.text:
Toolbox menu files are stored in the Menu/ directory in XML format.

Directory structure:

Toolbox installation directory/
├── AedtToolbox.exe
├── Menu/
│   ├── Default.xml              (base template, applies to all designs)
│   ├── CircuitDesign.xml        (Circuit design specific)
│   ├── HFSS.xml                 (HFSS design specific)
│   ├── HFSS-IE.xml              (HFSS-IE design specific)
│   ├── HFSSIE.xml               (HFSS IE design specific)
│   ├── Maxwell2D.xml            (Maxwell 2D design specific)
│   ├── Maxwell3D.xml            (Maxwell 3D design specific)
│   ├── HFSS3DLayoutDesign.xml   (3D Layout design specific)
│   ├── Icepak.xml               (Icepak design specific)
│   ├── Q2DExtractor.xml         (Q2D Extractor design specific)
│   ├── Q3DExtractor.xml         (Q3D Extractor design specific)
│   ├── TwinBuilder.xml          (TwinBuilder design specific)
│   └── EMIT.xml                 (EMIT design specific)
└── ...

Menu file types:

File Purpose Priority Default.xml Common menu for all design types Base {DesignType}.xml Design-specific menu (e.g. HFSS.xml, Maxwell3D.xml) Higher

docs.title: Menu Loading Logic
docs.text: Toolbox uses a smart menu merge mechanism to dynamically load and merge menus based on the currently active AEDT design type.

docs.title: Loading Flow
docs.text:
User right-clicks Toolbox
    ↓
contextMenuStrip1_Opening event fires
    ↓
Determine menu path
    ├─ Detect active AEDT window
    ├─ Query active design type
    ├─ Generate menu filename (e.g. Menu/HFSS.xml)
    └─ Check if file exists
    ↓
Load and merge menus
    ├─ Load {DesignType}.xml (ordering authority)
    ├─ Load Menu/Default.xml (content supplement)
    └─ Merge via MergeXmlNodes()
    ↓
MergeXmlNodes merge rules
    ├─ {DesignType}.xml order is the reference
    ├─ Items from Default.xml fill missing positions
    ├─ Relative order from Default.xml is preserved
    └─ Recursively merges sub-menus
    ↓
generateMenu() builds UI
    └─ Creates ToolStripMenuItems and binds click events

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
1. Design type detection - Automatically detects the active AEDT design type - Falls back to Default.xml if no design-specific file exists

2. Merge rules - Order authority — {DesignType}.xml determines display order - Content priority — {DesignType}.xml definitions take precedence - Auto supplement — Default.xml items are inserted at missing positions - Relative position preserved — Supplemented items keep their relative order from Default.xml

Example:

Default.xml order:  Help → SyncServer → About → Exit
HFSS.xml order:     Tool → Toolkits → Exit

Merged result:      Tool → Toolkits → Help → SyncServer → About → Exit
                    ↑       ↑         ↑        ↑           ↑       ↑
                  HFSS    HFSS    Default   Default      Default Default

3. Live reload After editing any XML file in the Menu/ directory, Toolbox automatically reloads on the next menu open — no restart required.

docs.title: Menu Format
docs.text:
<Menu>
    <SubMenu Name="test">
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="showProjectName_python"
                 ExecuteType="Python" Path="$UserLib/Template/showProjectName.py"
                 Arguments="" PythonPath="" LogWindow="True"/>
        <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="showProjectName_Ironpython"
                 ExecuteType="IronPython" Path="$UserLib/Template/showProjectName.py"
                 Arguments="" EntryFunc="main" PythonPath=""/>
    </SubMenu>
</Menu>

docs.title: Parameter Reference
docs.text: Parameter Description Name Display name of the menu item Type MenuItem (default), Separator ExecuteType Python, IronPython, EXE (Command is reserved for internal use) Path Script or executable path. Prefix with $ for relative paths (e.g. $UserLib/…) Arguments Arguments passed to the script, space-separated PythonPath Override Python executable path (optional) EntryFunc Entry function name (default: main) LogWindow True (default) keeps log window open; False hides it

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Spaces and Chinese characters in file paths may cause execution errors — avoid them
  • Use \\ or / for path separators in XML
  • XML syntax must be valid; syntax errors prevent menu loading
  • XML comments are known to cause loading errors — do not use comments in XML files
  • After saving edited XML, Toolbox reloads automatically

docs.title: Adding an EXE File
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="Notepad" ExecuteType="EXE"
         Path="Notepad.exe" Arguments=""/>
Parameter Description Name Menu display name ExecuteType EXE Path Executable path. Can also specify a document (e.g. xxx.docx) if the OS has a default handler Arguments Arguments, space-separated LogWindow Whether to show log window

docs.title: Adding a Python Script
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt"
         Arguments="" PythonPath=""/>
Parameter Description Name Menu display name ExecuteType Python Path Script path; use $ prefix for relative paths Arguments Optional; arguments passed to the script PythonPath Optional; override Python executable path EntryFunc Optional; entry function (default: main) LogWindow Show log window on execution

docs.title: Adding an IronPython Script
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="IronPython"
         Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt"
         Arguments="" PythonPath=""/>
Parameter Description Name Menu display name ExecuteType IronPython Path Script path; use $ prefix for relative paths Arguments Optional; arguments passed to the script PythonPath Optional; override IronPython executable path EntryFunc Optional; entry function (default: main) LogWindow Show log window on execution

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
• Close → QuitAedt: Force quit AEDT
  • Close → CloseProjectAndSave: Close current project and save
  • Close → CloseProjectNotSave: Close current project without saving
  • Close → CloseAllProjectAndSave: Close all projects and save
  • Close → CloseAllProjectNotSave: Close all projects without saving
  • Close → ReloadProject: Reload the current project

docs.title: Adding UDM / UDP Scripts
docs.text:
UDM and UDP enable more complex model construction and can be used in HFSS, Icepak, Maxwell 3D, and Q3D. The tool supports adding UDM and UDP scripts to the menu as follows:

<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="UDPTest" ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="xxxx/geodesic_sphere.py" EntryFunc="$UDP" LogWindow="False"/>
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="UDMTest" ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="xxxx/OnDieSpiralInductor.py" EntryFunc="$UDM" LogWindow="False"/>

docs.title: Parameter Description
docs.text:
When EntryFunc is set to $UDM, the tool treats the script as a UDM script and sends it to the corresponding AEDT window to execute the UDM command. When EntryFunc is set to $UDP, the tool treats the script as a UDP script and sends it to the corresponding AEDT window to execute the UDP command. For release builds, set LogWindow="False" to prevent the log window from appearing. For other parameters, refer to the Write your Script documentation.

UDM and UDP scripts are written following the standard AEDT script guide — save as .py files and place them anywhere. Relative paths with $ prefix can be used for files within the tool directory. The execution behavior is identical to clicking the menu item in AEDT software — a settings dialog will appear.

  1. Select the corresponding UDP or UDM script from the right-click menu

  2. The UDP/UDM settings dialog appears

Note: The tool automatically adds a UDMScript/UDPScript entry under UDM/UDP as an entry point. If removed, it will be regenerated. (If the user lacks write permissions to the sysLib directory, manually add UDMScript/UDPScript to the UserLib directory.)

docs.title: Python Script Development
docs.text:
Python scripts can be developed normally. Wrap the functionality into a function and specify it as the entry function in the XML menu configuration. The default entry function is main() — using main() as the entry point is recommended.

The example below defines multiple functions in one Python file. Different functions can be specified as entry points in the XML to achieve different actions. Alternatively, the same function can handle different behaviors via Arguments.

import sys, os

Module = sys.modules['__main__']
if hasattr(Module, "oDesktop"):
    oDesktop = getattr(Module, "oDesktop")
else:
    raise Exception("oDesktop initialization error.")


def closeAndSave():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    oProject.Save()
    oProject.Close()

def closeNotSave():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    oProject.Close()  # Unsaved changes will be lost.

def closeAllProjectWithSave():
    projects = oDesktop.GetProjects()
    for oProject in projects:
        oProject.Save()
        oProject.Close()

def closeAllProjectWithoutSave():
    projects = oDesktop.GetProjects()
    for oProject in projects:
        oProject.Close()

def ForceQuitAedt():
    oDesktop.QuitApplication()

def Reload():
    oProject = oDesktop.GetActiveProject()
    aedtPath = os.path.join(oProject.GetPath(), oProject.GetName() + ".aedt")
    oProject.Close()
    print("Reload aedt: %s" % aedtPath)
    oDesktop.OpenProject(aedtPath)

XML menu configuration:

<SubMenu Name="Close">
    <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="QuitAedt" ExecuteType="Python"
             Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="ForceQuitAedt"/>
    <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="CloseProjectAndSave" ExecuteType="Python"
             Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="closeAndSave"/>
    <SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="CloseProjectNotSave" ExecuteType="Python"
             Path="$UserLib/Desktop/Close.py" EntryFunc="closeNotSave"/>
</SubMenu>

docs.title: Download pyaedt Offline Package
docs.text: The tool requires pyaedt to be installed. If network access is restricted and pip installation is not possible, download the offline package for your Python version from: https://github.com/ansys/pyaedt

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: addPyaedtPanels is a standardized PyAEDT panel installer with a GUI that simplifies PyAEDT configuration and integration on Windows. It supports installing PyAEDT from multiple sources and automatically integrates it into the AEDT design environment.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• OS: Windows
  • Python: 3.10 or higher
  • AEDT: 2023.2 or higher
  • Network: Supports both online and offline (wheel file) installation

docs.title: Tool Location
docs.text:
Toolbox installation directory/
├── UserLib/
│   └── Toolkits/
│       └── addPyaedtPanels/
│           ├── install_pyaedt_gui.py       (main GUI application)
│           ├── refactor_utils.py           (shared utility functions)
│           └── *.pdf                       (user guide)
└── ...

docs.title: 1. Virtual Environment Management (create_venv)
docs.text:
• Creates a dedicated Python 3.10 virtual environment
  • Location: %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\ (Windows)
  • Detects existing environments to avoid re-creation
  • Isolated dependency management — does not affect system Python

docs.title: Online Installation (install_pyaedt_online)
docs.text:
• Installs the latest version with automatic dependency resolution
  • Requires network access

docs.title: Offline Installation (install_pyaedt_from_wheel)
docs.text:
• No network required; fast installation
  • Requires pre-downloaded .whl or .zip file
  • Ideal for corporate networks and multi-machine deployment

docs.title: 3. AEDT Panel Registration (add_pyaedt_to_aedt)
docs.text:
• Registers PyAEDT as AEDT design panels
  • Calls PyAEDT's official installer script
  • Auto-creates PersonalLib directory if missing

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
Launch install_pyaedt_gui.py
    ↓
Detect AEDT environment
    ├─ Running inside AEDT → relaunch with AEDT CPython 3.10
    └─ Standalone → continue with system Python 3.10+
    ↓
GUI opens
    ├─ Select installation method (online / offline)
    ├─ Manage virtual environment
    ├─ Monitor installation progress
    └─ Result feedback
    ↓
Create virtual environment
    └─ At %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\
    ↓
Install PyAEDT
    ├─ Online: pip install pyaedt[all]
    └─ Offline: pip install --find-links=<path>
    ↓
Register AEDT panels
    └─ ansys.aedt.core.extensions.installer.pyaedt_installer
    ↓
Complete — restart AEDT to apply changes

docs.title: Python Environment Priority
docs.text:
The installed venv integrates with Toolbox's Python detection hierarchy:

Priority Source Path 1 (highest) Custom user venv %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\3_10\Scripts\python.exe 2 System PATH python.exe in PATH 3 AEDT bundled ANSYSEM_ROOT\...\CPython\3_10\...\python.exe 4 (lowest) IronPython ANSYSEM_ROOT\common\IronPython

docs.title: AEDT Root Detection
docs.text:
The tool auto-scans environment variables for AEDT installations:

# Supported variable formats (case-insensitive):
AnsysEM_ROOT241    # AEDT 2024.1
ANSYSEM_ROOT232    # AEDT 2023.2

Multiple versions are sorted descending — the newest version is used.

docs.title: GUI Launch Methods
docs.text:
Method 1: Via Toolbox menu → select "PyAEDT Panel Installer"

Method 2: Command line:

python install_pyaedt_gui.py

Method 3: AEDT IronPython console:

exec(open(r"C:\...\addPyaedtPanels\install_pyaedt_gui.py").read())

docs.title: Toolbox Menu Integration
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem" Name="Install PyAEDT Panel"
         ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib/Toolkits/addPyaedtPanels/install_pyaedt_gui.py"
         EntryFunc="main" />

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Cause Solution "AEDT Python not found" AEDT version too old Upgrade AEDT to 2023.2+ Permission denied No write access to venv path Modify %APPDATA% permissions or change install path Installation timeout Unstable network Use offline wheel installation or retry PersonalLib not found AEDT hasn't created default dir Handled automatically — no action needed

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Do not modify install_pyaedt_gui.py — it is generated from a standard template
  2. Restart AEDT after installing to load the new panels
  3. Permissions: Write access to %APPDATA% is required
  4. Uninstall: Delete %APPDATA%\.pyaedt_env\ to fully remove the virtual environment

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
q2d_TlineGenerator is a quick modeling tool for Ansys Q2D Extractor. It automatically generates a parameterized multi-line transmission-line cross-section. The tool provides a minimal GUI where the user specifies the line count, and it then creates a new 2D Extractor design in AEDT with conductors, dielectrics, materials, surface roughness settings, and default solution setup.

Typical use cases include:

  • Quickly building parallel transmission-line Q2D cross-sections
  • Creating a reusable starting template for impedance, coupling, and RLGC analysis
  • Running parameter studies on line width, line spacing, and dielectric values

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Runtime: Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT)
  • Design Type: Q2D Extractor
  • Execution Mode: Python menu script
  • GUI Dependency: Tkinter

docs.title: Tool Location
docs.text:
Toolbox installation directory/
├── UserLib/
│   └── Toolkits/
│       └── q2d_TlineGenerator/
│           └── TlineGenerator.py    (main script)
└── ...

docs.title: Menu Configuration
docs.text:
<SubMenu Type="MenuItem"
         Name="q2d_TlineGenerator"
         ExecuteType="Python"
         Path="$UserLib\toolkits\q2d_TlineGenerator\TlineGenerator.py" />

docs.title: Interface
docs.text:
When the tool starts, it opens a small dialog with the following controls:

  • Line Count: Number of transmission lines to generate
  • OK: Starts model creation
  • Cancel: Closes the dialog without creating a model

docs.title: Input Rules
docs.text:
• Only positive integers are accepted
  • The default value is 4
  • If the value is empty, non-integer, or less than or equal to 0, the tool shows an error dialog

docs.title: Automatically Generated Content
docs.text: After OK is clicked, the tool inserts a new 2D Extractor design into the current AEDT project and creates the following items.

docs.title: 1. Parameter Variables
docs.text: The tool creates a group of local variables based on the requested line count so the generated model can be tuned later inside AEDT.

docs.title: Width and Spacing
docs.text:
• w_line1_top ~ w_lineN_top: Top width of each line
  • w_line1_bot ~ w_lineN_bot: Bottom width of each line
  • s_line1_line2 ~ s_line(N-1)_lineN: Spacing between adjacent lines
  • s_left, s_right: Left and right edge margins

docs.title: Thickness and Dielectric Parameters
docs.text:
• T_gnd_top, T_gnd_bot: Top and bottom reference-plane thickness
  • T_line: Trace thickness
  • H_core: Lower core dielectric thickness
  • H_pp: Upper prepreg dielectric thickness

docs.title: Auto-Positioning Variables
docs.text:
The tool also creates the following position variables to place each line automatically:

  • line1_left, line1_right
  • line2_left, line2_right
  • ...
  • lineN_left, lineN_right
  • max_right: Total width boundary of the full cross-section

docs.title: 2. Material Parameters
docs.text:
Project-level material variables are created so dielectric and conductor values can be edited in one place:

  • $pp_dk, $core_dk, $fill_dk
  • $pp_df, $core_df, $fill_df
  • $cond_line, $cond_top_gnd, $cond_bot_gnd

Default examples:

  • Relative permittivity: 3.6
  • Loss tangent: 0.008
  • Conductivity: 5.8e7

docs.title: 3. Geometry Objects
docs.text:
The tool creates the following objects in the 3D Modeler:

  • tline1 ~ tlineN: Parameterized transmission-line conductors
  • gnd_bot: Bottom reference ground
  • gnd_top: Top reference ground
  • core: Core dielectric
  • fill: Fill dielectric around the traces
  • pp: Upper prepreg dielectric

Behavior details:

  • Each line uses a trapezoidal cross-section so top and bottom widths can differ
  • Ground width follows max_right automatically
  • Dielectric and conductor regions are positioned by variables for later optimization

docs.title: 4. Surface Roughness Model
docs.text: The tool assigns Huray surface roughness parameters to both signal lines and ground planes.

docs.title: Trace Roughness Variables
docs.text:
• huray_radius_line_left
  • huray_radius_line_top
  • huray_radius_line_right
  • huray_radius_line_bot
  • huray_ratio_line_left
  • huray_ratio_line_top
  • huray_ratio_line_right
  • huray_ratio_line_bot

docs.title: Ground Roughness Variables
docs.text:
• huray_radius_gnd_top
  • huray_radius_gnd_bot
  • huray_ratio_gnd_top
  • huray_ratio_gnd_bot

The default roughness radius is 0.5um and the default ratio is 2.9.

docs.title: 5. Boundary and Solution Setup
docs.text:
The tool also completes these Q2D settings automatically:

  • Assigns gnd_bot and gnd_top as reference grounds
  • Assigns each tlineX as an independent signal line
  • Creates analysis setup Setup1
  • Sets the adaptive frequency to 10GHz
  • Inserts frequency sweep Sweep1

docs.title: Default Sweep Range
docs.text:
The sweep uses two logarithmic ranges:

  • 1Hz to 1GHz
  • 1GHz to 100GHz

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
Launch q2d_TlineGenerator
    ↓
Open the input dialog
    ↓
Enter Line Count
    ↓
Create a new 2D Extractor design
    ↓
Create local and project variables
    ↓
Create dielectric and conductor materials
    ↓
Generate transmission lines, grounds, and dielectric layers
    ↓
Assign signal and ground boundaries
    ↓
Apply the Huray roughness model
    ↓
Insert Setup1 and Sweep1
    ↓
Finish parameterized model initialization

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Open the target project in AEDT
  2. Launch q2d_TlineGenerator from the Toolbox menu
  3. Enter the required line count, such as 4, 8, or 16
  4. Click OK to generate the initial model
  5. Edit line width, spacing, dielectric thickness, and material parameters in the new Q2D design
  6. Run simulation or create Optimetrics sweeps as needed

docs.title: Common Variables to Tune Afterwards
docs.text:
• w_line*_top, w_line*_bot
  • s_line*_line*
  • T_line
  • H_core, H_pp
  • $core_dk, $pp_dk, $fill_dk

docs.title: Parallel Bus Cross-Section Modeling
docs.text: When evaluating coupling, characteristic impedance, or loss across multiple parallel traces, this tool can generate a standardized starting cross-section quickly and then be refined by adjusting widths and spacing.

docs.title: Process Window Studies
docs.text: Because the top and bottom widths are independent, the tool is also suitable for modeling etched trapezoidal cross-sections and studying how fabrication variation affects impedance and crosstalk.

docs.title: Roughness Sensitivity Studies
docs.text: By changing the Huray-related variables, the tool can be used to study how conductor roughness impacts high-frequency loss and RLGC parameters.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Each execution inserts a new 2D Extractor design into the current project
  2. The tool builds a parameterized model only; it does not automatically start the solver
  3. If no active AEDT desktop context is found, the script attempts to initialize one through its external interface path
  4. Larger line counts will increase the later boundary, mesh, and solution scale significantly
  5. After creation, check whether the default variable values match the intended board material stack and copper thickness

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Cause Suggested Fix No model is generated after launch No valid AEDT session is available Launch the tool from Toolbox or inside an AEDT session Invalid input message appears Line Count is not a positive integer Enter an integer greater than 0 Geometry is created but dimensions are incorrect Default variables do not match the design target Review and adjust w_line*, s_left/right, H_core, and related variables High-frequency loss is too large or too small Roughness values do not match the actual process Adjust the Huray radius and ratio parameters
